claim: A focused agent is a performant agent; context engineering (managing what enters the context window) is more important than model choice
actionable: Audit your agent's context window daily using /context; remove wasteful MCP servers, trim bloated memory files, and use context priming instead
source_title: Elite Context Engineering with Claude Code

claim: Default MCP.json files waste 12-20% of context window; delete them and load MCPs manually only when needed for specific tasks
actionable: Remove MCP.json; use `claude --mcp config <specific-config>` to load targeted MCPs per task, saving 20k+ tokens
source_title: Elite Context Engineering with Claude Code

claim: Large claude.md files (3000+ lines) that auto-load create static, unmanageable context; keep it under 50 lines and use context priming instead
actionable: Trim claude.md to only universal essentials (42 lines max); use dynamic /prime commands for task-specific context loading
source_title: Elite Context Engineering with Claude Code

claim: Context priming—using reusable /prime commands with Purpose/Run/Read/Report structure—gives agents dynamic focus without bloating memory
actionable: Create /prime bug, /prime feature, /prime chore commands that load only relevant context for specific workflows
source_title: Elite Context Engineering with Claude Code

claim: Sub-agents use system prompts (not context-loaded); each spawned agent gets isolated context, keeping primary agent's window clean (delegation pattern)
actionable: Delegate context-heavy work (web scraping, doc analysis) to sub-agents instead of loading everything in primary agent
source_title: Elite Context Engineering with Claude Code

claim: Context bundles (session-based logs of agent work) allow follow-up agents to resume context efficiently without replaying entire histories
actionable: Use /loadbundle to replay previous agent context in new instance; tracks session ID and prunes stale reads/writes
source_title: Elite Context Engineering with Claude Code

claim: R&D framework: Reduce context entering primary agent + Delegate work to sub-agents; this is the only way to manage context at scale
actionable: Every context engineering technique fits into Reduce or Delegate; audit your system against these two goals
source_title: Elite Context Engineering with Claude Code

claim: Background agent delegation via /background runs out-of-loop agents for specialized work (planning, scraping); frees primary agent from waiting
actionable: Use background agents for reports, plans, and long-running tasks; primary agent continues without blocking
source_title: Elite Context Engineering with Claude Code

claim: Management of context window is more critical than adding new capabilities; performance degrades with context bloat before feature gains appear
actionable: Measure context usage weekly; prefer removing waste over adding features when deciding where to invest engineering time
source_title: Elite Context Engineering with Claude Code

claim: Optimal context window usage is 0%; engineers should minimize deterministic context by maximizing agent model weights' latent knowledge
actionable: Question every context addition: can the model already know this? Only load context unavailable in model weights
source_title: Elite Context Engineering with Claude Code
